channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Silenced voices: Imo school for the deaf where students battle neglect, rape
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Explore the tragic reality of Imo State Secondary School for the Deaf, where students face neglect, insecurity, and alleged rape amidst crumbling infrastru
Read More: https://punchng.com/silenced-voices-imo-school-for-the-deaf-where-students-battle-neglect-rape/

item.title: ‘Let court decide Gbajabiamila case on fake agency’
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
PDP chieftain urges Nigerians to let the court decide on the Femi Gbajabiamila case regarding the alleged fake Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion
Read More: https://punchng.com/let-court-decide-gbajabiamila-case-on-fake-agency/

item.title: Court upholds FCCPC’s powers to probe airline ticket pricing
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A Federal High Court has affirmed the FCCPC’s powers to investigate consumer complaints regarding airline ticket pricing, a major victory for consumer prot
Read More: https://punchng.com/court-upholds-fccpcs-powers-to-probe-airline-ticket-pricing/

item.title: I want to put Nigeria on global map with CopaceticMusic — BYTunes
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigerian artist BYTunes discusses his move to Los Angeles to globalize CopaceticMusic, a new genre blending diverse sounds while preserving its Nigerian ro
Read More: https://punchng.com/i-want-to-put-nigeria-on-global-map-with-copaceticmusic-bytunes/

item.title: ‘Cross River to complete dialysis centre’
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Cross River State approves funding to complete a kidney dialysis centre at Ogoja General Hospital, expanding access to vital renal healthcare services.
Read More: https://punchng.com/cross-river-to-complete-dialysis-centre/

item.title: ADC, PDP shun Edo LG polls, 12 parties participate
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The African Democratic Congress (ADC) and Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) will not participate in the Edo LG polls, while 12 other parties are set to contes
Read More: https://punchng.com/adc-pdp-shun-edo-lg-polls-12-parties-participate/

item.title: Division biggest threat to Church, says Enenche
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Pastor Paul Enenche warns that division is the greatest threat to the Church’s survival, urging Nigerian Christians to unite against insecurity and persecu
Read More: https://punchng.com/division-biggest-threat-to-church-says-enenche/

item.title: Being married, divorced or single is an achievement!
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
In Nigeria, every marital status—single, married, or divorced—is an achievement requiring immense courage. Discover why your marital status is a triumph.
Read More: https://punchng.com/being-married-divorced-or-single-is-an-achievement/

item.title: Police rescue four Abuja kidnap victims
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
FCT Police successfully rescued four Abuja kidnap victims after a fierce gun battle in Mpape. One resident died, but all hostages are safe.
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-rescue-four-abuja-kidnap-victims/

item.title: Sam Larry’s car crash reignites Mohbad debate
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Sam Larry’s bodyguard died in a car crash, sparking renewed debate and ‘karma’ claims linking the incident to Mohbad’s controversial death.
Read More: https://punchng.com/sam-larrys-car-crash-reignites-mohbad-debate/

item.title: Escape to serene hideaway of Grand Bassam
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The West African coastline from Nigeria down to Mauritania is fascinatingly beautiful, and steeped in the intrigue of history. Along the coastline are small, big towns and cities. Each of these has stories to tell. Stories of the past that echo betrayals, intrigues and valour. Years after those who

item.title: OPay pushes AI as Africa’s next financial inclusion growth engine
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay Tech
item.description: OPay has declared that the next phase of Africa’s fintech revolution will be driven by Artificial Intelligence (AI), arguing that read more OPay pushes AI as Africa’s next financial inclusion growth engine

item.title: As Tony Elumelu closes prodigious chapter
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Like a colossus bestriding Nigeria’s banking landscape, Tony O. Elumelu has stood out among his contemporaries for more than three decades. For the influential investor, his foundation has been built in banking and financial services, where governance, balance-sheet strength, and regulatory complian

item.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Meta told Dylan Byers, of Puck News, that it had nixed the feature after backlash from its user base.

item.title: YAYI possesses the capacity, leadership qualities to govern Ogun — Akintunde
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Convener of Yayi Progressives Movement in Ogun State, Armstrong ‘Tope Akintunde, in this interview speaks on Nigeria’s current security challenges, President Bola Tinubu’s economic reforms, Governor Dapo Abiodun’s Senate ambition, and why Yewa should produce the next governor of Ogun State, among ot

item.title: Meta turns off the Instagram feature that let users make AI deepfakes of public accounts
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Following significant backlash, Meta is turning off the feature it announced this week that let users generate AI images based on content from public Instagram accounts just by tagging them. The feature, as originally set up, meant that content from any public Instagram account could be used in AI c

item.title: My husband threw my belongings out because I refused to abort a pregnancy — Wife
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A WOMAN from Kitwe has narrated in Bulangililo Local Court how her husband promised to buy her hungry Lion pack if she agreed to terminate her pregnancy. The post My husband threw my belongings out because I refused to abort a pregnancy — Wife appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Deaconess ‘Lola Olabode hits diamond in style
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Businesswoman and philanthropist, Deaconess Grace Lola Olabode has built a reputation for refined character and community service. She is widely respected across her community and described by many as a beacon of hope and dedication. The post Deaconess ‘Lola Olabode hits diamond in style appeared fi

item.title: Nigeria: Breaking - Kidnapped Oyo Pupils, Teachers Freed
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Premium Times] The pupils and teachers abducted from three schools in Oyo State have been freed, PREMIUM TIMES can report.

item.title: Inside Babatunde Okewale’s three decades of medical excellence
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Healthcare tycoon, Dr. Babatunde Okewale has every reason to celebrate. When in 1996 he abandoned a promising swish life in the United Kingdom as an obstetrician and gynaecologist to return home to start his clinic and improve on the practice of reproductive health, Lagos, Nigeria didn’t give him a

item.title: Nicaragua strips lawyers of certification in latest crackdown on dissent
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The government of Daniel Ortega and Rosario Murillo has been accused of human rights abuses against critics.

item.title: Otunba Funso Lawal glides into 71 with grace
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: In the oil and gas sector, Otunba Funso Lawal has steadily carved a niche for himself, earning admiration as one of the industry’s dependable figures. Through decades of changing economic fortunes, shifting policies and fierce competition, the Chairman of Sogenal Petroleum has remained steadfast, bu

item.title: Merino the unlikely hero again - as Spain wait for Lamine Yamal show
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Mikel Merino came off the bench to send Spain into the World Cup semi-finals - but Lamine Yamal is still waiting for his moment to truly shine.

item.title: Nigeria: APC Retains Shettima As Tinubu's Running Mate Ahead of 2027 Election
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [Premium Times] The formal unveiling effectively dispels months of speculation that President Tinubu could replace Mr Shettima with a Northern Christian in response to concerns surrounding the party's Muslim-Muslim ticket.

item.title: 79 years of grace and impact: Family, friends, political, business leaders celebrate Chief Eunice Uzor Kalu at 79
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: It was a day of elegance, gratitude, family affection and national recognition as political leaders, captains of industry, diplomats, legal luminaries, clergy, business executives and admirers from across Nigeria gathered to celebrate the 79th birthday of Elder Chief (Mrs.) Eunice Uzor Kalu, fondly

item.title: BammyJohn Stitches founder expands footprint in contemporary African fashion
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: As African fashion continues to gain global recognition for its innovation, craftsmanship and cultural influence, Fatoba Bamidele John, Founder and Creative Director of BammyJohn Stitches, is positioning his brand among the growing number of labels contributing to the industry's evolution. The post

item.title: NYSC: The last institution where the son of nobody becomes somebody
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: By: Sunday Adewale By all standards, reform is a necessary ingredient of institutional survival. No public institution can remain effective in a rapidly changing society without periodic review, adaptation, and renewal. It is therefore understandable that the Federal Government has initiated a compr

item.title: Cuba suffers second island-wide blackout in a week amid Trump fuel blockade
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US President Donald Trump has placed the Caribbean island under a de facto oil blockade, straining its power grid.

item.title: South Sudan: South Sudan - UN Acts Ahead of Drought to Protect Thousands
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [UN News] As drought risk intensifies in South Sudan's Eastern Equatoria state, a new UN-backed plan aims to protect thousands of people before the worst impacts take hold.

item.title: Lammens' error spells end for Belgium's golden generation
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Belgium's World Cup campaign ends after Senne Lammens' error against Spain - could it all be over for their golden generation too?

item.title: World Scoliosis Day: Experts push for greater awareness, early diagnosis to save lives
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Yet, medical experts warn that such seemingly minor signs could indicate scoliosis, a spinal condition that, if left undetected, can progress into severe deformity, chronic pain, breathing difficulties and significant psychological distress. The post World Scoliosis Day: Experts push for greater awa

item.title: How Anambra women in US empowered breast cancer survivors
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The guests, mostly Anambra women in Lagos, came to bear testimony to the unflagging zeal of the Anambra State Association, Women in USA, Inc. (ASA Women USA), for their fight against breast cancer. The post How Anambra women in US empowered breast cancer survivors appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Rising costs, falling sales: How Nigeria’s economic hardship crushes small traders
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: On a typical trading day at Bakin Dogo, the expanded fruit section of Kaduna Central Market, located near the Government House, Kaduna Local Government area of Kaduna State, the careful display of watermelons, bananas and pineapples attracts curious buyers despite the current economic realities in t

item.title: Apple sues OpenAI for alleged theft of confidential info — and says that’s just ‘the tip of the iceberg’
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The iPhone maker claims “misconduct is normalized and exemplified by leadership” at OpenAI.

item.title: I fed our five children for 2yrs after my husband deserted us — Wife
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: •I was ill, receiving treatment —Husband A house wife, Naja’atu Lawal, who dragged her husband, Isa Yusuf before a Sharia Court sitting in Magajin Gari, Kaduna State, alleged that he requested to see her and give her divorce papers, but upon meeting with him, he tore her hijab and rained insults on

item.title: Meet the crypto billionaires building a world where money buys you a vote
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: They think democracy has run its course - so they're setting up their own nations instead.

item.title: Forced to wait his turn, Marine Le Pen's deputy Bardella returns to the shadows
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Thirty-year-old Jordan Bardella had been groomed for success as France's next hard-right leader. He will now have to wait longer.

item.title: MGM Resorts Engaged in Deal Talks With Diller, WSJ Says
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: MGM Resorts International is engaged in deal talks with Barry Diller’s People Inc. after the entertainment mogul offered to acquire the part of the casino company he doesn’t already own, the Wall Street Journal reported.

item.title: Apple files lawsuit accusing ChatGPT maker OpenAI of stealing trade secrets
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The complaint alleges a coordinated effort by two former Apple employees and OpenAI to steal confidential information.

item.title: ECB face no action over Stokes retirement video
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: No action will be taken by the International Cricket Council (ICC) over Ben Stokes' retirement video.

item.title: Apple sues OpenAI, its employees claiming theft of trade secrets
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Apple said in a Friday lawsuit that OpenAI’s nascent hardware business is “rotten to its core.”

item.title: S&P Global: FIFA to Offer Short-Term Economic Boost
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Nora Wittstruck, Chief Analytical Officer for Governments at S&P Global, joined Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on Bloomberg's 'The Close' to provide insights on FIFA's financial position during the 2026 World Cup. She highlights how FIFA has emerged financially stronger despite facing numero

item.title: Mutfwang Commissions Potato Value Chain Projects, Distributes Agricultural Equipment
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Plateau State Governor, Barr. Caleb Manasseh Mutfwang on Friday commissioned the Potato Value Chain Project, a state-of-the-art Tissue Culture Laboratory and the distribution of modern agricultural equipment in Mangu Local Government Area. ‎ ‎The initiative is a landmark investment that will transfo

item.title: “Nigeria Has Never Experienced Advancements We’re Seeing Under Tinubu” — Ooni of Ife
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The Ooni of Ife, Oba Adeyeye Enitan Ogunwusi, Ojaja II, has hailed President Bola Tinubu’s administration, saying Nigeria is witnessing unprecedented progress under his leadership. Speaking at the inauguration of the PBAT Door-to-Door Movement in Abuja on Thursday, the monarch commended Tinubu’s eco

item.title: Colombia’s Jaminton Campaz receives death threats after World Cup exit
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Campaz was threatened after failing to score in the last-16 match against Switzerland, which Colombia lost on penalties.

item.title: Pro Shop's Chad Mumm on 'Full Swing,' Golf in 2026
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Chad Mumm, Co-Founder of Pro Shop and Producer of the Netflix series 'Full Swing,' talks about the world's number-one ranked golfer, Scottie Scheffler, missing the cut at the Scottish Open. He also discusses golf's surge in popularity and the anticipation for the upcoming season of 'Full Swing.' Mum

item.title: Kaduna 2027: The ADC’s defining test, By Jacob Ibrahim Ochocho
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: If the party truly seeks to maximise its chances in 2027, its National Working Committee should respect the findings of its own internal appeal mechanism regarding the governorship primary election The post Kaduna 2027: The ADC’s defining test, By Jacob Ibrahim Ochocho appeared first on Premiu

item.title: Man fatally shot by ICE in Houston was not intended target, DHS says
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Immigration agents were looking for a different person when they shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo during a traffic stop, officials say.

item.title: BEYOND THE SHARE: WHY STRENGTHENING ELECTORAL INTEGRITY BEGINS WITH RESPONSIBLE COMMUNICATION
item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: By John Best Uche In an era where information travels faster than verification, the future of democracy may well depend on the choices we make before clicking the “Post” button. From 6–8 July 2026, I had the privilege of representing The post BEYOND THE SHARE: WHY STRENGTHENING ELECTORAL

item.title: Under Lula, Amazon deforestation falls to lowest level in a decade
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The figures come months before Brazil’s presidential election, with Lula promising to end illegal deforestation by 2030.

item.title: The FCC is cracking down on DJI tech that dodged the foreign drone ban
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Last year, we told you about Xtra, the company that lets DJI sneak its popular cameras into the US, and Skyrover, a brand seemingly selling DJI drones in disguise. They're just two of the many firms DJI is suspected of starting to skirt the United States' foreign drone ban. But it appears the FCC is

item.title: Keyamo: Obi refused to pay fine after violating airport parking rules
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Minister of Aviation and Aerospace Development, Mr Festus Keyamo, SAN, has demanded a public apology from the Nigerian Democratic Congress (NDC) Presidential Candidate, Peter Obi, over claims that his vehicle was unfairly clamped at the Nnamdi Azikiwe International Airport, Abuja. The Minister while

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Oyo Renaissance Crusade (ORC) has expressed profound appreciation to the Government of Oyo State, the nation’s security agencies, and all stakeholders whose concerted efforts culminated in the safe release of the teachers and schoolchildren who were abducted in the Orire Local Government A

item.title: Meta’s stock roars back to life as it notches its best week in years
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Investors are bullish on Meta’s new low-cost AI pricing and infrastructure plans.

item.title: Security Expert Reveals How Intelligence, Technology Led to Rescue of Oyo Pupils, Teachers
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: A security expert and former Department of State Services (DSS) operative, Dr Seyi Adetayo, has revealed fresh details about the security operation that led to the rescue of the abducted pupils and teachers from Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State, saying the mission was driven by intelligence

item.title: Northern Christians Laud Tinubu’s Nomination Of Shettima As Running Mate
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Middlebelt Pentecostal Association (MPA) has warmly welcomed President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s decision to retain Vice President Kashim Shettima as his running mate for the 2027 presidential election. The group described the move as a demonstration of continuity, stability, and commitment to nationa

item.title: SK Hynix’s stock sees double-digit pop in Nasdaq debut
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The South Korean memory-chip company’s new American depositary receipts began trading on Friday and logged gains of nearly 13%.

item.title: Appeal Court rejects jailed former accountant-general’s bail request
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The former accountant-general of the federation, who is serving an eight-year jail time, sought the bail pending appeal. The post Appeal Court rejects jailed former accountant-general’s bail request appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: NYSC: Rep Writes Tinubu, Says Reforms Require More Input From Stakeholders
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: A member of the House of Representatives, Hon. Philip Agbese, has called for the suspension of the sweeping reforms to the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) recently approved by the Federal Executive Council (FEC). Agbese, representing Ado/Okpokwu/Ogbadibo Federal Constituency and Deputy Spokesman

item.title: Canada’s Bill C-36 tackles AI privacy. Is it enough?
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Canada’s new privacy bill promises stronger protections for children, but experts say it still misses risks in the AI world.

item.title: World Cup: Merino’s late winner sends Spain past Belgium into semi-finals
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Spain has set up a blockbuster semi-final clash with France in Dallas on 14 July. The post World Cup: Merino’s late winner sends Spain past Belgium into semi-finals appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: England's Guehi, Rice & James all train before Norway game
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Midfielder Declan Rice and defenders Marc Guehi and Reece James all train with England on the eve of Saturday's World Cup quarter-final against Norway.

item.title: Benue: Alia focused on long-term development, not palliatives, supporter says
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: ABUJA — A supporter of Benue State Governor Hyacinth Alia, Tagher Tagher Wan U Tiv, popularly known as Baba Atsar Gundu, has defended the governor’s development approach, saying the administration is prioritising long-term economic empowerment over dependence on palliatives. In a statement mad

item.title: Spain into semi-finals at expense of Belgium after Merino's late winner
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Mikel Merino emerges as Spain's hero yet again as the substitute scores in the 88th minute to stun Belgium and set up a World Cup semi-final against France.

item.title: Bloomberg Money 7/10/2026
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Bloomberg Money takes the pulse of your financial life, powered by the reporting of our global newsroom. Today's guests are: University of Chicago Booth School Professor of Economics and Former Fed Governor Randall Kroszner and NewEdge Wealth CIO Cameron Dawson. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Obi not bigger than law, must apologise, pay N25,000 fine for wrong parking — Keyamo
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The Minister of Aviation and Aerospace Development, Festus Keyamo, SAN, has demanded that the presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, Peter Obi, apologise to airport officials and pay a N25,000 fine over an alleged parking violation at the Nnamdi Azikiwe International Airport

item.title: Merino: From mobility scooter to World Cup hero in six months
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Mikel Merino might have hoped to appear earlier than the 85th minute against Belgium following his match-winning impact from the bench against Portugal. Instead, he had to wait until the 86th. No matter. Another late cameo, another decisive goal.

item.title: SK Hynix Debut Is a Bet That AI Breaks Boom-and-Bust Chip Cycle
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: South Korean memory chipmaker SK Hynix Inc. just pulled off the largest public listing by a foreign company in US market history. Shares soared 13% on their first day of trading.

item.title: Bluesky’s interim CEO, Toni Schneider, drops the ‘interim’
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Schneider, who formerly served as the CEO of Automattic and is a partner at True Ventures, says he is "all in" on the unconventional social media platform.

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: England received a boost on the eve of their World Cup quarter-final against Norway after Declan Rice, Marc Guehi and Reece James all returned to training on Friday. The post England’s Rice, Guehi and James train ahead of Norway World Cup clash appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Sinner, Zverev Power Into Wimbledon Final
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Sinner is within touching distance of a second successive Wimbledon title after ending Djokovic's latest attempt to win a record 25th Grand Slam triumph. The post Sinner, Zverev Power Into Wimbledon Final appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Rescue of Oyo schoolchildren: S-West Fulani chiefs hail Tinubu, Makinde, security agencies
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Dapo Akinrefon The Council of Fulani Chiefs in South West Nigeria has commended President Bola Tinubu, Oyo State Governor Seyi Makinde and security agencies for the successful rescue of schoolchildren and teachers abducted in Oriire Local Government Area of the state. The council, led by its Chai

item.title: World Cup 2026: Merino Strikes Late Again As Spain Edge Out Belgium, Set Up Semifinal Clash With France
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Arsenal star Mikel Merino was the match winner once again as his late strike for Spain helped them to a 2-1 win against Belgium in the FIFA World Cup 2026 quarterfinal on Friday. With two minutes left in the game Merino, who was the match winner in the round of 16 against Portugal, pounced on [...]

item.title: Nuclear Energy Services Firm Holtec Joins Sector’s IPO Rush
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Holtec Nuclear Corp. filed for a US initial public offering, becoming the latest in the sector to try to capitalize on data centers’ growing power demands.

item.title: Spain leave it late to book semifinal date with France
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Mikel Merino comes off the bench to break Belgian hearts after slip from sub keeper Senne Lammens.

item.title: Rhode Island Jail Operator Files Bankruptcy to Cut Bond Debt
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Central Falls Detention Facility Corp., which houses federal detainees including people arrested by the US Immigration and Customs Enforcement agency, filed for bankruptcy saying it cannot afford to keep paying on more than $167 million in bond debt.

item.title: PHOTOS: The Operation that rescued Oyo pupils, teachers
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Nigerian security forces have secured release of the pupils and teachers who were abducted by Ansaru terrorists in Oyo State, on May 15, 2026. The intelligence led Search and Rescue operation, which concluded on 9 July 2026, followed a direct presidential mandate and was executed by a Defence Headqu

The

item.description: By Henry Umoru ABUJA — Chairman of the Senate Committee on Ethics, Code of Conduct and Public Petitions, Senator Neda Imasuen (APC, Edo South), has denied allegations linking him to fraud, bank robbery and criminal investigations in the United States, describing the claims as a politically motivated

item.title: New dinosaur species with neck as long as cricket pitch discovered in Thailand
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The plant-eating dinosaur, named Uragasaurus kalasinensis, is thought to have lived about 150 million years ago.

item.title: World Cup: Merino winner sends Spain to semi-final
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Spain scored a late winner to defeat Belgium 2-1 and progress into the semi-final of the 2026 FIFA World Cup on Friday. Mikel Merino was the difference maker for Spain netting the winner two minutes before the end of 90 minutes after Fabian Ruiz’s opener was cancelled by Charles De Ketelaere. Spain

item.title: ‘I’d hate to end up with an unexpected tax bill’: I’m 73 and still work full time. Can I avoid paying taxes on my Social Security benefits?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “I’m actually earning more each week than I ever have before.”

item.title: ‎“I Was Body-Shamed For Being Fat; Still Body-Shamed For Losing Weight” – Eniola Badmus
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: Nollywood actress Eniola Badmus is celebrating herself on her weight loss journey. ‎In a lengthy post on her Instagram page, she disclosed that five years ago she weighed 170kg and today weighs 83kg. She said it didn’t happen overnight and took years of discipline, sacrifice, pain, consistency, tear

item.title: Obasa Commends Tinubu, Security Agencies Over Rescue Of Abducted Oyo Pupils
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Speaker of the Lagos State House of Assembly, Rt. Hon. Mudashiru Obasa, has expressed immense joy and relief following the successful rescue of the kidnapped Oyo State schoolchildren and their teachers on the eve of Friday, July 10, 2026. In a statement issued by his Chief Press Secretary, Mr. Dave

item.title: Gov Eno Sympathises With Flood Victims, Urges Residents To Keep Drainages Clear
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Eno acknowledged the government's responsibility to respond to flooding but stressed that residents also have a duty to maintain their immediate environment. The post Gov Eno Sympathises With Flood Victims, Urges Residents To Keep Drainages Clear appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Gov Yusuf Urges Nigerians To Support Tinubu’s Second-Term Ambition
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The Governor also commended Vice President Kashim Shettima and the leadership of the APC for their commitment to party unity and a credible electoral process. The post Gov Yusuf Urges Nigerians To Support Tinubu’s Second-Term Ambition appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Shionogi-Backed Apnimed Files for IPO to Fund Sleep Apnea Drug
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Apnimed Inc. filed for a US initial public offering, as its oral drug to treat obstructive sleep apnea moves closer to being commercialized.

item.title: Oriire victims’ rescue: kidnapping cannot thrive in Yorubaland — Oluwo
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The monarch described the activities of kidnappers and bandits as unacceptable, particularly in the South-West, insisting that criminal elements would not... The post Oriire victims’ rescue: kidnapping cannot thrive in Yorubaland — Oluwo appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Released Oyo kidnapped students: New security framework needed – Shehu Sani
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Kaduna Central Senator, Shehu Sani, has said a new security framework is needed to protect students when they return to school. Sani made the remark while commending the National Security Adviser, NSA, Nuhu Ribadu; military, the Department of State Services, DSS; and the police over the relea

item.title: Fubara presents N1.854trn Rivers 2026 budget
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Governor Fubara on Friday presented a proposed N1.854 trillion 2026 Appropriation Bill to the Rivers State House of Assembly, with... The post Fubara presents N1.854trn Rivers 2026 budget appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Attention should now be shifted to Borno – Ndume reacts to rescued Oyo schoolchildren, teachers
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Borno South Senator, Ali Ndume, has reacted to the rescue of schoolchildren and teachers who were abducted from Oyo state. Featuring in an interview on Channels Television’s Politics Today on Friday, Ndume said attention should now be shifted to Borno and his constituency in particular. DAILY

item.title: Fintiri Urges Journalists To Embrace Responsible, Peace-Oriented Reporting
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: He warned that the growing spread of misinformation and disinformation continues to fuel tensions and undermine peaceful coexistence. The post Fintiri Urges Journalists To Embrace Responsible, Peace-Oriented Reporting appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Nigeria stocks mint N9.34trn in seven-day buying spree
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: In a spectacular display of bullish dominance, the Nigerian equities market rebounded aggressively in the trading week to Friday July read more Nigeria stocks mint N9.34trn in seven-day buying spree
